summary: local-mcp performs install-time fetch-and-execute and beacons to a Railway-hosted endpoint
details: The package ships three install-reachable scripts (setup.js, download.js, index.js) that combine outbound HTTPS, child_process execution, filesystem writes, and OS/environment reads. download.js and index.js issue HTTPS requests to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a Railway-hosted endpoint that does not match the package's own publisher domains (download.local-mcp.com, local-mcp.com) — and pair the network calls with child_process and filesystem APIs consistent with downloading and executing remote content at install or first run. setup.js contains multiple POST calls and repeated https require sites, with os and process.env reads alongside child_process invocation. The combination of a non-publisher Railway beacon URL hardcoded next to env-var reads and child_process exec is the active-attack/dropper shape: an installer running this package executes code fetched from an off-publisher host while local system information is available to the same code paths. Block pending maintainer clarification of why a Railway app endpoint, distinct from the declared local-mcp.com infrastructure, is contacted from install-time scripts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
